sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports back pain following a 2020 microdiscectomy, with pain exacerbated by bending over and random pain episodes. He reports pain on the left side around L5 and reports no leg pain. He describes pain as horrible and nerve-like. He denies pain with flexion and extension. He reports a muscle pull from misalignment.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals normal dorsiflexion, pain with palpation on the left side around L5, and no pain with palpation on the right side. X-ray shows no fractures or spinal bone abnormalities.
medical_concepts: pain with palpation on the left side around L5
pain with palpation on the right side

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has persistent left-sided low back pain likely due to a muscle strain secondary to spinal misalignment following a previous microdiscectomy.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe physical therapy referral to the VA, prescribe meloxicam instead of ibuprofen, and prescribe demerol. Arrange a consultation with pain management.

medical_concepts: pain with palpation on the left side around L5
pain with palpation on the right side
